edges.description: The developmental renal arterial lesion is the defining renal artery stenosis or deficiency phenotype.
Stenotic or absent renal arterial inflow lowers renal perfusion pressure.
Reduced renal arterial perfusion produces secondary systemic hypertension through renovascular physiology.
Longstanding renal vascular disease can compromise renal growth or function, producing a small or atrophic affected kidney.
Reduced renal perfusion activates endocrine blood-pressure responses.
Persistent RAAS-driven hypertension increases cardiac afterload and remodeling risk.
edges.target: Left ventricular remodeling from renovascular hypertension

edges.description: Persistent pediatric renovascular hypertension increases cardiac afterload and produces left ventricular hypertrophic remodeling.
edges.source: Left ventricular remodeling from renovascular hypertension

nodes.description: Congenital renal artery variants, including stenosis, atresia, hypoplasia, or absence, create a small-caliber or absent inflow vessel to the kidney. This structural lesion limits renal perfusion and establishes the anatomic substrate for renovascular hypertension.

evidence.snippet: The wide range of renal vascular diseases include congenital renal artery and vein variations, aneurysms, arteriovenous malformations (AVMs), renal artery stenosis, renal vein thrombosis, vasculitis, and traumatic injuries, such as dissection and vascular pedicle injury.

evidence.reference_title: Renal Artery Reconstruction for Refractory Hypertension Caused by Congenital Renal Artery Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: He was diagnosed with a congenital absence of the right renal artery.

nodes.description: Chronic hypoperfusion may leave the affected kidney small or functionally compromised.

evidence.reference_title: Whole exome sequencing identified monogenic causes of midaortic syndrome in a large cohort of patients.
evidence.snippet: Morbidity is high in severe cases, with patients at considerable risk for acute events, such as hypertensive encephalopathy, stroke, progressive cardiac and renal dysfunction.

nodes.description: Chronic renovascular hypertension can cause pediatric cardiac remodeling including increased left ventricular mass.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiovascular outcomes improve in children with renovascular hypertension following endovascular and surgical interventions.
evidence.snippet: A common cause of RenoVH is renal artery stenosis which acts by reducing blood supply to renal parenchyma and activating the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone axis, often leading to cardiac remodelling.

nodes.description: Severe renovascular hypertension can remodel the pediatric left ventricle and improve after successful intervention.
nodes.id: Left ventricular remodeling from renovascular hypertension

evidence.reference_title: Cardiovascular outcomes improve in children with renovascular hypertension following endovascular and surgical interventions.
evidence.snippet: Average z-scores improved in interventricular septal thickness in diastole (IVSD), posterior Wall thickness in diastole (PWD) and fractional shortening (FS); left ventricular mass index (LVMI) and relative wall thickness (RWT) also improved.

nodes.description: Congenital narrowing or deficiency of the renal artery is the defining vascular lesion.

evidence.reference_title: Interventions in children with renovascular hypertension: A 27-year retrospective single-center experience.
evidence.snippet: Vascular involvement ranged from MAS with RAS (20), RAS only (32), and MAS only (1).

nodes.description: Reduced perfusion of the affected kidney can activate pressure-sensitive renal endocrine responses and produce secondary systemic hypertension. In children, renal artery stenosis may occur alone or with middle aortic syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Interventions in children with renovascular hypertension: A 27-year retrospective single-center experience.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Renovascular hypertension (RVH) can be caused by renal artery stenosis (RAS) and/or middle aortic syndrome (MAS).

evidence.reference_title: Renal Artery Reconstruction for Refractory Hypertension Caused by Congenital Renal Artery Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Renovascular hypertension is a common cause of secondary hypertension.

nodes.description: Reduced renal perfusion from renal artery stenosis activates the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone axis, promoting severe or refractory hypertension and cardiac remodeling in children.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiovascular outcomes improve in children with renovascular hypertension following endovascular and surgical interventions.
evidence.snippet: A common cause of RenoVH is renal artery stenosis which acts by reducing blood supply to renal parenchyma and activating the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone axis, often leading to cardiac remodelling.

nodes.description: Hypertension is the major clinical presentation when renal arterial stenosis reduces kidney perfusion.

evidence.reference_title: Renal Artery Reconstruction for Refractory Hypertension Caused by Congenital Renal Artery Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The right renal function was recovered, and the blood pressure was well controlled after the Aorta-Right Renal Artery Bypass.
